rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core codes.audio fix and produce grounded, non-fabricated findings, but X's top non-blocking comment ('containment fallback now applies to all speech tests, not just opt-in ones... globally loosens every caller') closely mirrors the GT reviewer's central concern, and X goes further with a concrete counterexample (expected 'hello' passing against transcript 'hello world goodbye') plus a specific actionable fix (gate behind opt-in or tighten small_word_delta). Y's findings are mostly correct but skew toward low-value nitpicks (dead guard, extract magic numbers, add docstring detail) and only tangentially touch the GT's legacy-audio-ordering concern via the get/pop asymmetry note. Neither candidate matches GT's exact 'update the docstring/module comment' ask verbatim, but X's independent discovery of the same underlying risk is sharper and more useful than Y's broader but shallower nitpick list.
